# Frontier Agent Source Map

Converted from `55_frontier_agent_source_map.html` so the pack remains Markdown-only.

```html
<!doctype html><html lang='en'><head><meta charset='utf-8'><title>Frontier Agent Source Map</title><style>body{font-family:Arial,sans-serif;line-height:1.45;margin:2rem;max-width:1100px}table{border-collapse:collapse;width:100%}td,th{border:1px solid #ddd;padding:.5rem;vertical-align:top}th{background:#f7f7f7}</style></head><body><h1>Frontier Agent Source Map</h1><p>Clickable source map for Claude Code, Opus 4.7, coding-agent benchmarks, and benchmark criticism.</p><table><tr><th>ID</th><th>Title</th><th>Type</th><th>Why read</th><th>Bias notes</th></tr><tr><td>anthropic-opus-47-news</td><td><a href='https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-7'>Introducing Claude Opus 4.7</a></td><td>official announcement</td><td>Launch narrative, partner claims, Opus 4.7 positioning around long-running coding, agents, vision, and knowledge work.</td><td>Vendor source. Good for release facts and claimed improvements; weak for independent model comparison.</td></tr><tr><td>anthropic-opus-model-page</td><td><a href='https://www.anthropic.com/claude/opus'>Claude Opus model page</a></td><td>official product page</td><td>Product positioning and partner comments for Opus 4.7.</td><td>Vendor source; treat quotes as early adopter evidence, not neutral benchmarking.</td></tr><tr><td>anthropic-models-overview</td><td><a href='https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview'>Claude API models overview</a></td><td>official docs</td><td>Current model IDs, pricing, context window, output limits, model-selection guidance.</td><td>Official technical docs; accurate for Anthropic platform facts, not independent performance validation.</td></tr><tr><td>anthropic-opus-47-whats-new</td><td><a href='https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/whats-new-claude-4-7'>What is new in Claude Opus 4.7</a></td><td>official docs</td><td>Breaking API changes, adaptive thinking, xhigh effort, task budgets, image resolution, tokenizer changes.</td><td>Official technical docs; strong for implementation details.</td></tr><tr><td>anthropic-system-cards</td><td><a href='https://www.anthropic.com/system-cards'>Anthropic model system cards</a></td><td>official system card index</td><td>Canonical location for Claude Opus 4.7 System Card and safety/capability evals.</td><td>Vendor disclosure document; useful but not independently reproducible by default.</td></tr><tr><td>claude-code-overview</td><td><a href='https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview'>Claude Code overview</a></td><td>official docs</td><td>Core Claude Code concepts: instructions, skills, hooks, project context.</td><td>Official docs; strong for usage features.</td></tr><tr><td>claude-code-how-works</td><td><a href='https://code.claude.com/docs/en/how-claude-code-works'>How Claude Code works</a></td><td>official docs</td><td>Agentic loop, tools, context, sessions, permissions, checkpoints, branch workflows.</td><td>Official docs; strong for architecture and operating model.</td></tr><tr><td>claude-code-best-practices</td><td><a href='https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices'>Best practices for Claude Code</a></td><td>official docs</td><td>Explore-plan-code-commit workflow, CLAUDE.md, verification, CLI tools, MCP, hooks, skills, subagents.</td><td>Official guidance; practical but Anthropic-specific.</td></tr><tr><td>claude-code-common-workflows</td><td><a href='https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows'>Claude Code common workflows</a></td><td>official docs</td><td>Codebase exploration, debugging, refactoring, testing, PRs, session management.</td><td>Official docs; implementation oriented.</td></tr><tr><td>swebench-main</td><td><a href='https://www.swebench.com/'>SWE-bench official leaderboards and benchmark family</a></td><td>benchmark</td><td>Canonical software-engineering benchmark family: Verified, Full, Multilingual, Multimodal, Lite.</td><td>Public benchmark. Useful, but leaderboard metrics can be harness-sensitive and may not match your repo.</td></tr><tr><td>swebench-paper</td><td><a href='https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.06770'>SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?</a></td><td>paper</td><td>Original benchmark paper; explains task format and real GitHub issue setup.</td><td>Academic benchmark; important baseline but Python/repo distribution limits generality.</td></tr><tr><td>swebench-multilingual</td><td><a href='https://www.swebench.com/multilingual.html'>SWE-bench Multilingual</a></td><td>benchmark</td><td>Extends SWE-bench style evaluation beyond Python across 9 languages.</td><td>Better language coverage, still benchmark-specific.</td></tr><tr><td>swebench-pro</td><td><a href='https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.16941'>SWE-Bench Pro: Can AI Agents Solve Long-Horizon Software Engineering Tasks?</a></td><td>paper/benchmark</td><td>Harder long-horizon coding-agent evaluation; important for production-like claims.</td><td>More realistic than basic tasks, still not your production environment.</td></tr><tr><td>terminal-bench</td><td><a href='https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.11868'>Terminal-Bench 2.0</a></td><td>paper/benchmark</td><td>Hard terminal-environment tasks with 89 curated tasks; relevant to CLI/agent workflows.</td><td>Good for command-line agency; not a direct proxy for product/marketing/webapp work.</td></tr><tr><td>aider-polyglot</td><td><a href='https://aider.chat/docs/leaderboards/'>Aider LLM leaderboards and Polyglot benchmark</a></td><td>benchmark</td><td>Practical code-editing benchmark across C++, Go, Java, JavaScript, Python, and Rust.</td><td>Tool/harness-specific; good for Aider-like editing, not complete software engineering.</td></tr><tr><td>utboost-swebench</td><td><a href='https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.189/'>UTBoost: Rigorous Evaluation of Coding Agents on SWE-Bench</a></td><td>paper</td><td>Shows some SWE-bench tasks have insufficient tests and that leaderboard rankings can change after test augmentation.</td><td>Important corrective paper; do not use it to dismiss SWE-bench entirely.</td></tr><tr><td>data-contamination-survey</td><td><a href='https://arxiv.org/html/2502.14425'>A Survey on Data Contamination for Large Language Models</a></td><td>paper</td><td>Explains benchmark contamination and mitigation strategies.</td><td>Survey; broad rather than coding-agent-specific.</td></tr><tr><td>llm-benchmark-survey</td><td><a href='https://arxiv.org/html/2508.15361'>A Survey on Large Language Model Benchmarks</a></td><td>paper</td><td>Catalogues benchmark limitations: contamination, cultural/language bias, missing process credibility and dynamic environments.</td><td>Survey; useful for meta-evaluation discipline.</td></tr><tr><td>swebench-live</td><td><a href='https://openreview.net/forum?id=OGWkr7gXka'>SWE-bench Goes Live!</a></td><td>paper/benchmark</td><td>Live-updatable benchmark built from newer GitHub issues to reduce staleness and contamination.</td><td>Promising direction; still benchmark-dependent.</td></tr><tr><td>prodcodebench</td><td><a href='https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01527'>ProdCodeBench: A Production-Derived Benchmark for Evaluating AI Coding Agents</a></td><td>paper</td><td>Methodology for building benchmark tasks from real production assistant sessions, including tests and stability checks.</td><td>Best read as methodology for your own eval, not as universal benchmark truth.</td></tr><tr><td>metaculus-metr-productivity</td><td><a href='https://metr.org/'>METR / AI productivity studies</a></td><td>research area</td><td>Use as a gateway for external work on AI productivity and task completion, especially when benchmark gains do not translate to human productivity.</td><td>Research org; check individual study design before generalising.</td></tr></table></body></html>
```
